summary: 美国与伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡附近交火后同意暂时停火，并计划在卡塔尔会晤。伊朗袭击未命中目标，特朗普威胁伊朗将'不复存在'。同时，国会质疑87.6亿美元补充战争资金，民主党借战争不受欢迎施压。此外，委内瑞拉地震救出婴儿，犹他州山火肆虐，美国队备战世界杯。

vocabulary.cn: 退出，停止（军事行动或对峙）
vocabulary.en: to withdraw or retreat from a position or conflict
vocabulary.excerpt: Both the U.S. and Iran agreeing to meet in Qatar tomorrow after exchanging fire near the Strait of Hormuz. Both the U.S. and Iran agreeing to meet in Qatar tomorrow after exchanging fire near the Strait of Hormuz.
vocabulary.example_cn: 美国和伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡附近交火后，同意明天在卡塔尔会晤。

vocabulary.en: a temporary suspension of fighting, typically one during which peace talks take place
vocabulary.excerpt: Is this ceasefire collapsing?
vocabulary.example_cn: 这个停火协议正在瓦解吗？

vocabulary.en: waste or rough fragments of stone, brick, concrete, etc., especially after demolition or an earthquake
vocabulary.excerpt: Babies buried under piles of debris found alive, days after dual mass earthquakes in Venezuela.
vocabulary.example_cn: 委内瑞拉发生双重大地震数天后，埋在瓦砾堆下的婴儿被发现还活着。

vocabulary.en: extremely hot weather
vocabulary.excerpt: And fires turn deadly in Utah. Now, scorching heat is about to make things a little harder for firefighters.
vocabulary.example_cn: 犹他州的火灾变得致命。现在，酷热将使消防员的工作更加困难。

vocabulary.en: a competitor thought to have little chance of winning a fight or contest
vocabulary.excerpt: And everyone loves an underdog. Does the U.S. have a shot at winning the World Cup?
vocabulary.example_cn: 每个人都喜欢弱者。美国队有机会赢得世界杯吗？

vocabulary.cn: 扫荡行动，清理残余敌人的军事行动
vocabulary.en: a military operation to clear an area of remaining enemy forces after a major battle
vocabulary.excerpt: The major war is over. And think of this as almost just a mop-up operation.
vocabulary.example_cn: 主要战争已经结束。把这几乎看作是一次扫荡行动。

vocabulary.en: an agreement between enemies or opponents to stop fighting for a certain time
vocabulary.excerpt: Could the truce be on the verge of crumbling?
vocabulary.example_cn: 休战协议是否濒临瓦解？

vocabulary.en: very close to; about to
vocabulary.excerpt: Could the truce be on the verge of crumbling?
vocabulary.example_cn: 休战协议是否濒临瓦解？

vocabulary.cn: 霍尔木兹海峡（连接波斯湾和阿拉伯海的重要石油运输通道）
vocabulary.en: a narrow strait between the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman, a vital oil shipping route
vocabulary.excerpt: Both the U.S. and Iran agreeing to meet in Qatar tomorrow after exchanging fire near the Strait of Hormuz.
vocabulary.example_cn: 美国和伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡附近交火后，同意明天在卡塔尔会晤。

vocabulary.en: the targets that were planned to be hit
vocabulary.excerpt: but a U.S. official says no Iranian drones or missiles hit their intended targets
vocabulary.example_cn: 但一名美国官员表示，伊朗的无人机或导弹没有击中预定目标。

vocabulary.en: breaking or failing to comply with (a rule or formal agreement)
vocabulary.excerpt: President Trump threatened that, if Iran continued violating the ceasefire, it, quote, 'will no longer exist.'
vocabulary.example_cn: 特朗普总统威胁说，如果伊朗继续违反停火协议，伊朗将'不复存在'。

vocabulary.en: the transport of goods by sea between countries
vocabulary.excerpt: while Iran continues to attack international shipping without a response
vocabulary.example_cn: 而伊朗继续攻击国际航运而不受回应。

vocabulary.en: the basic physical and organizational structures needed for the operation of a society or enterprise
vocabulary.excerpt: take down their infrastructure that they're trying to use to illegally control an international waterway
vocabulary.example_cn: 摧毁他们试图用来非法控制国际水道的基础设施。

vocabulary.en: short, unplanned fights, especially between small groups of soldiers
vocabulary.excerpt: They are OK with the level of violence and these skirmishes, these exchange of attacks
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们接受这种程度的暴力和这些小规模冲突、这些攻击的交换。

vocabulary.en: a figurative line that should not be crossed, especially in diplomacy or conflict
vocabulary.excerpt: as they try and set their red lines and try and shape negotiations
vocabulary.example_cn: 当他们试图设定红线并试图塑造谈判时。

vocabulary.en: the period of time that can be predicted with reasonable certainty
vocabulary.excerpt: I think we're going to see these for the foreseeable future.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我认为在可预见的未来我们还会看到这些。

vocabulary.en: the most important and practical details of a subject or situation
vocabulary.excerpt: And I'm under the impression this is the part that really gets into the nitty gritty of what happens to enriched uranium.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我的印象是，这部分真正涉及浓缩铀去向的实质细节。

vocabulary.cn: 浓缩铀（铀-235含量提高的铀，可用于核反应堆或核武器）
vocabulary.en: uranium in which the proportion of the isotope uranium-235 has been increased, used as fuel in nuclear reactors or in nuclear weapons
vocabulary.excerpt: this is the part that really gets into the nitty gritty of what happens to enriched uranium.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这部分真正涉及浓缩铀去向的实质细节。

vocabulary.en: to use power or influence to control something
vocabulary.excerpt: you see Iran trying to exert its control over the Strait of Hormuz
vocabulary.example_cn: 你看到伊朗试图对霍尔木兹海峡施加控制。

vocabulary.en: the power to influence a person or situation to achieve a desired outcome
vocabulary.excerpt: Iran is trying to retain leverage over the Strait of Hormuz, its major lever that it has had during this war
vocabulary.example_cn: 伊朗试图保留对霍尔木兹海峡的影响力，这是它在战争期间拥有的主要杠杆。

vocabulary.en: things that are granted or yielded, especially in negotiations
vocabulary.excerpt: whether or not Iran, through this process, is actually getting concessions.
vocabulary.example_cn: 伊朗是否通过这一过程实际上获得了让步。

vocabulary.en: an unexpected, unearned, or sudden gain or advantage
vocabulary.excerpt: because the Iranians want to keep getting the windfall that they've gotten out of this MOU.
vocabulary.example_cn: 因为伊朗人想继续从这份谅解备忘录中获得意外之财。

vocabulary.en: a document describing a bilateral or multilateral agreement between parties, not legally binding
vocabulary.excerpt: the windfall that they've gotten out of this MOU.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们从这份谅解备忘录中获得的意外之财。

vocabulary.cn: 补充战争资金（额外拨款的军事预算）
vocabulary.en: additional money allocated by a government to cover the costs of a war beyond the regular budget
vocabulary.excerpt: the U.S. wants to be at the table, but they're asking for supplemental war funding of Congress to do it to the tune of 87.6 billion.
vocabulary.example_cn: 美国想留在谈判桌上，但他们要求国会提供876亿美元的补充战争资金。

sentences.en: Both the U.S. and Iran agreeing to meet in Qatar tomorrow after exchanging fire near the Strait of Hormuz.
sentences.cn: 美国和伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡附近交火后，同意明天在卡塔尔会面。
sentences.structure: 主句：Both the U.S. and Iran agreeing to meet in Qatar tomorrow；时间状语：after exchanging fire near the Strait of Hormuz。
sentences.analysis: 现在分词短语作主句谓语，表示主动和进行；after + 动名词短语作时间状语，表示先后顺序。

sentences.en: Iran targeted U.S. allies in Bahrain and Kuwait, but a U.S. official says no Iranian drones or missiles hit their intended targets, saying, quote, "To be clear, Iran failed."
sentences.cn: 伊朗袭击了美国在巴林和科威特的盟友，但一名美国官员表示，没有伊朗无人机或导弹击中预定目标，并引用说：“明确地说，伊朗失败了。”
sentences.structure: 并列句：Iran targeted...but a U.S. official says...；saying...为现在分词短语作伴随状语，quote引导直接引语。
sentences.analysis: but连接转折关系；saying...补充说明官员的言论；quote用于新闻中引入直接引语。

sentences.en: President Trump threatened that, if Iran continued violating the ceasefire, it, quote, "will no longer exist."
sentences.cn: 特朗普总统威胁说，如果伊朗继续违反停火协议，它“将不复存在”。
sentences.structure: 主句：President Trump threatened that...；宾语从句：if Iran continued violating the ceasefire, it will no longer exist；插入语：quote。
sentences.analysis: if引导条件状语从句；quote表示直接引用；"will no longer exist"是威胁性表述。

sentences.en: If the Iranian regime thinks for a second that President Trump is going to sit -- sit by, stand by while Iran continues to attack international shipping without a response or our bases without a response, they're sadly mistaken.
sentences.cn: 如果伊朗政权有一秒钟认为特朗普总统会袖手旁观，坐视伊朗继续攻击国际航运或我们的基地而不作出回应，那他们就大错特错了。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句：If the Iranian regime thinks...；宾语从句：that President Trump is going to sit by, stand by...；主句：they're sadly mistaken。
sentences.analysis: sit by和stand by同义重复，强调不作为；while引导时间状语从句；without a response作状语修饰attack。

sentences.en: And they saw that loud and clear over the last few nights, where we'll continue to militarily, if needed, take down their infrastructure that they're trying to use to illegally control an international waterway.
sentences.cn: 他们在过去几个晚上清楚地看到了这一点，我们将继续在必要时采取军事行动，摧毁他们试图用来非法控制国际水道的设施。
sentences.structure: 主句：And they saw that loud and clear over the last few nights；where引导非限制性定语从句修饰nights；从句中if needed为插入条件状语；that引导定语从句修饰infrastructure。
sentences.analysis: loud and clear为固定短语，意为“清楚地”；militarily为副词修饰take down；if needed为省略句。

sentences.en: I think both Tehran and Washington have decided that the ceasefire is more fire than cease.
sentences.cn: 我认为德黑兰和华盛顿都认定，这场停火与其说是停火，不如说是交火。
sentences.structure: 主句：I think；宾语从句：both Tehran and Washington have decided that...；that引导的宾语从句中：the ceasefire is more fire than cease。
sentences.analysis: more...than...结构表示“与其说……不如说……”，fire和cease形成对比，fire指交火，cease指停止。

sentences.en: They are OK with the level of violence and these skirmishes, these exchange of attacks, as they try and set their red lines and try and shape negotiations, frankly, via these attacks.
sentences.cn: 他们接受这种程度的暴力和这些小规模冲突、这些攻击交换，因为他们试图通过这些攻击来设定红线并塑造谈判，坦率地说。
sentences.structure: 主句：They are OK with...；as引导原因状语从句；从句中try and set...and try and shape...为并列谓语；frankly为插入语。
sentences.analysis: be OK with表示“接受”；skirmishes和exchange of attacks为同位语；via these attacks作方式状语。

sentences.en: And right now, there is a commitment to continue talking at the same time that they're going to continue shooting.
sentences.cn: 而现在，他们承诺继续谈判，同时他们也将继续开火。
sentences.structure: 主句：there is a commitment to continue talking；at the same time that引导时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: commitment to do sth.表示“做某事的承诺”；at the same time that表示“在……的同时”。

sentences.en: And I'm under the impression this is the part that really gets into the nitty gritty of what happens to enriched uranium.
sentences.cn: 我的印象是，这部分真正涉及浓缩铀去向的具体细节。
sentences.structure: 主句：I'm under the impression；同位语从句：this is the part...；that引导定语从句修饰part；of what happens...为介词短语作定语修饰nitty gritty。
sentences.analysis: under the impression that...意为“有……的印象”；nitty gritty为俚语，意为“具体细节”。

sentences.en: So, Iran is trying to retain leverage over the Strait of Hormuz, its major lever that it has had during this war, without --
sentences.cn: 因此，伊朗正试图保持对霍尔木兹海峡的影响力，这是它在战争期间拥有的主要杠杆，而无需——
sentences.structure: 主句：Iran is trying to retain leverage over the Strait of Hormuz；its major lever为同位语；that引导定语从句修饰lever；without后省略。
sentences.analysis: retain leverage over意为“保持对……的影响力”；lever比喻“杠杆，手段”。

sentences.en: The Iranians are leaning forward to exercise control over the strait to remind the world that they control that waterway, then, leaning back when the Trump administration objects vigorously enough, because the Iranians want to keep getting the windfall that they've gotten out of this MOU.
sentences.cn: 伊朗人向前倾，以行使对海峡的控制权，提醒世界他们控制着那条水道，然后，当特朗普政府足够强烈地反对时，他们又向后靠，因为伊朗人想继续从这份谅解备忘录中获得意外之财。
sentences.structure: 并列结构：leaning forward...then leaning back...；leaning forward后接不定式表目的；when引导时间状语从句；because引导原因状语从句；that引导定语从句修饰windfall。
sentences.analysis: lean forward/back比喻“向前/后退”；exercise control over意为“行使对……的控制”；windfall意为“意外之财”；MOU为Memorandum of Understanding缩写。

sentences.en: And then when it comes to the nuclear file, I think they will dribble out very small concessions bit by bit, then pull them back, then put them forward, then pull them back to keep the United States at the table.
sentences.cn: 然后当谈到核问题时，我认为他们会一点一点地挤出非常小的让步，然后收回，再提出，再收回，以让美国留在谈判桌上。
sentences.structure: 时间状语从句：when it comes to the nuclear file；主句：I think；宾语从句：they will dribble out...；并列谓语：dribble out, pull back, put forward, pull back；不定式表目的：to keep...。
sentences.analysis: when it comes to...意为“当谈到……”；dribble out意为“一点点地放出”；bit by bit意为“一点一点地”；keep...at the table意为“让……留在谈判桌上”。

sentences.en: Democrats are obviously very opposed to this to begin with, but I think the fraying of this ceasefire -- or not ceasefire or whatever you want to call it right now -- is just going to intensify questions in Congress about this huge amount of money they want.
sentences.cn: 民主党人显然从一开始就非常反对这一点，但我认为，这场停火——或者不是停火，或者你现在想怎么称呼它——的破裂只会加剧国会对他们想要这笔巨额资金的质疑。
sentences.structure: 并列句：Democrats are opposed...but I think...；think后宾语从句：the fraying...is going to intensify questions...；破折号内为插入语；they want为定语从句修饰money。
sentences.analysis: to begin with意为“首先，一开始”；fraying意为“磨损，破裂”；or not ceasefire or whatever为口语化插入语。

sentences.en: Democrats see blood in the water, and they're going to make them pay in November for this war that's unpopular across both parties.
sentences.cn: 民主党人看到了机会，他们要让共和党人在11月为这场在两党都不受欢迎的战争付出代价。
sentences.structure: 并列句：Democrats see blood...and they're going to make them pay...；that引导定语从句修饰war。
sentences.analysis: see blood in the water为习语，意为“看到机会（尤指对手的弱点）”；make sb. pay意为“让某人付出代价”；across both parties意为“在两党中”。

sentences.en: In our reporting -- and we write about this in the book -- none of Trump's senior advisors, none of his cabinet, really thought this was a good idea.
sentences.cn: 在我们的报道中——我们在书中也写到了这一点——特朗普的高级顾问、他的内阁成员，没有人真的认为这是个好主意。
sentences.structure: 主句：none of Trump's senior advisors...thought this was a good idea；破折号内为插入语。
sentences.analysis: none of...none of...双重否定强调无人；插入语补充说明。

sentences.en: But Vance was the only one who really vocally took issue with this, with Trump.
sentences.cn: 但万斯是唯一一个真正公开对此提出异议的人，与特朗普意见相左。
sentences.structure: 主句：But Vance was the only one；who引导定语从句修饰one；with Trump为状语。
sentences.analysis: vocally意为“直言地”；take issue with意为“对……提出异议”。

sentences.en: And it irritated Trump.
sentences.cn: 这激怒了特朗普。
sentences.structure: 简单句。
sentences.analysis: irritate意为“激怒”。

sentences.en: I think you're seeing a larger I mean, what she is talking about is a larger dynamic in this White House, is that people do not want to give Trump bad news.
sentences.cn: 我认为你看到的是一个更大的——我的意思是，她所谈论的是白宫中一个更大的动态，那就是人们不想给特朗普带来坏消息。
sentences.structure: 主句：I think；宾语从句：you're seeing a larger...；插入语：I mean；what引导主语从句；that引导表语从句。
sentences.analysis: I mean为口语插入语；dynamic意为“动态，互动模式”。

sentences.en: And you're seeing Trump having -- trotting -- trotting out Vance and being like, Well, now you fix it.
sentences.cn: 而且你看到特朗普让万斯出来——出来——然后说，好吧，现在你来解决它。
sentences.structure: 主句：you're seeing Trump having...；现在分词短语trotting out Vance作宾语补足语；being like引导直接引语。
sentences.analysis: trot out意为“推出，展示（常含贬义）”；being like为口语用法，意为“说”。

sentences.en: You didn't even want to go in, but now it's your responsibility to fix this thing.
sentences.cn: 你当初甚至不想介入，但现在解决这件事是你的责任。
sentences.structure: 并列句：You didn't even want to go in, but now it's your responsibility to fix this thing。
sentences.analysis: go in意为“介入”；it's one's responsibility to do sth.意为“做某事是某人的责任”。

topics.title: 美伊冲突与霍尔木兹海峡
topics.content: 霍尔木兹海峡（Strait of Hormuz）是连接波斯湾和阿拉伯海的狭窄水道，全球约20%的石油运输经过此处。伊朗常以控制该海峡作为地缘政治杠杆。2026年美伊冲突中，双方在附近交火后同意暂时停火，但伊朗试图通过限制航运路线维持影响力。
topics.keywords: Strait of Hormuz · ceasefire · leverage · international shipping

topics.title: 美国国会战争资金争议
topics.content: 美国行政分支向国会请求补充战争资金（supplemental war funding）用于军事行动。2026年美伊冲突中，特朗普政府要求876亿美元，但国会两党对此分歧严重。民主党借战争导致油价上涨、民众不满，在中期选举前施压共和党。
topics.keywords: supplemental war funding · Congress · gas prices · midterm elections

topics.title: 委内瑞拉双重地震与救援
topics.content: 2026年6月，委内瑞拉发生两次大规模地震（dual mass earthquakes），造成大量建筑倒塌。救援人员在废墟（rubble）中发现多名幸存婴儿，被称为'奇迹'。此类灾害凸显发展中国家应急响应能力的挑战。
topics.keywords: dual mass earthquakes · rubble · rescue · Venezuela

topics.title: 美国队世界杯前景
topics.content: 2026年世界杯淘汰赛阶段，美国队（Team USA）对阵波黑队（Bosnia）。作为underdog（不被看好的一方），美国队面临强敌，但此前表现令部分球迷惊喜。世界杯是全球最受关注的体育赛事之一，美国队晋级前景引发讨论。
topics.keywords: World Cup · underdog · Team USA · Bosnia

quiz.question: 原文中 'stand down' 的含义是什么？
quiz.explanation: 在军事语境中，'stand down' 意为退出或停止行动。原文 'Both the U.S. and Iran agreeing to stand down for now' 指双方同意暂时停止冲突。

quiz.question: 短语 'on the verge of' 在 'Could the truce be on the verge of crumbling?' 中的意思是？
quiz.explanation: 'on the verge of' 意为'濒临、即将'，表示某事即将发生。这里指休战协议濒临瓦解。

quiz.question: 原文中 'MOU' 的全称和含义是？
options: A. Memorandum of Understanding，谅解备忘录
C. Major Operation Unit，主要行动单位
D. Monetary Overhaul Unit，货币改革单位
quiz.explanation: MOU 是 Memorandum of Understanding 的缩写，指双方达成的非法律约束力的谅解备忘录。原文提到伊朗从 MOU 中获得 windfall（意外之财）。

quiz.question: 根据原文，美国与伊朗在交火后同意做什么？
quiz.explanation: 原文明确说 'Both the U.S. and Iran agreeing to meet in Qatar tomorrow after exchanging fire'，即同意在卡塔尔会晤。

quiz.question: 关于补充战争资金，国会民主党人的态度如何？
quiz.explanation: 原文提到 'Democrats see blood in the water, and they're going to make them pay in November'，说明民主党反对并利用战争不受欢迎来施压。

quiz.question: 根据 Maggie Haberman 的新书，特朗普的顾问们对战争的态度是？
options: B. 多数认为不是好主意，但只有万斯公开反对
quiz.explanation: 原文引用 Haberman 说 'none of Trump's senior advisors... really thought this was a good idea. But Vance was the only one who really vocally took issue'，即多数不赞成，但只有万斯公开反对。

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Anchor
paragraphs.text: In Nevada, the Air Force performed a flyover to kick off their Fourth of July celebration. The iconic Thunderbirds flew over famous landmarks including Las Vegas, Hoover Dam and red rock landmarks including Las Vegas, Hoover Dam and Red Rock Canyon. All right. Quite the sight to see there. That does it for CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Brad Smith. CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: 在内华达州，空军进行了一次飞越表演，拉开了独立日庆祝活动的序幕。标志性的雷鸟飞行队飞越了包括拉斯维加斯、胡佛水坝和红岩峡谷在内的著名地标。好吧，这景象真是壮观。CNN头条快报到此结束。我是布拉德·史密斯。奥黛·科尼什主持的CNN今晨节目现在开始。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Iran and the U.S. trade strikes. President Trump threatens Tehran will, quote, "no longer exist." Is this ceasefire collapsing?
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗和美国相互发动打击。特朗普总统威胁称，德黑兰将"不复存在"。停火协议正在瓦解吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Violence will be met with violence.
paragraphs.cn: 暴力将遭到暴力回击。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And miracles in the rubble. Babies buried under piles of debris found alive, days after dual mass earthquakes in Venezuela. And fires turn deadly in Utah. Now, scorching heat is about to make things a little harder for firefighters. And everyone loves an underdog. Does the U.S. have a shot at winning the World Cup?
paragraphs.cn: 废墟中的奇迹。委内瑞拉发生双重大地震数天后，埋在瓦砾堆下的婴儿被发现还活着。犹他州火灾致人死亡。现在，酷热将使消防员的工作更加艰难。人人都爱弱者。美国有机会赢得世界杯吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: They've been playing good. Like, at first, I was even skeptical.
paragraphs.cn: 他们一直打得不错。比如，起初我甚至持怀疑态度。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: I do support the U.S. Team. I think there's no chance. SEN. ROGER MARSHALL (R-KS): The major war is over. And think of this as almost just a mop-up operation. We have to press them. If they strike us, we have to strike them back by ten.
paragraphs.cn: 我确实支持美国队。但我觉得没机会。参议员罗杰·马歇尔（堪萨斯州共和党人）：主要战争已经结束。把这几乎看作一次扫尾行动。我们必须施压。如果他们打击我们，我们必须以十倍的力度回击。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, a fragile ceasefire back on after a fresh outburst of strikes between the U.S. and Iran. Could the truce be on the verge of crumbling? Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. We're going to start with the commitment to stand down for now. Both the U.S. and Iran agreeing to meet in Qatar tomorrow after exchanging fire near the Strait of Hormuz. Iran targeted U.S. allies in Bahrain and Kuwait, but a U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 那么，在美国和伊朗新一轮打击爆发后，脆弱的停火协议得以恢复。休战是否濒临崩溃？大家早上好。我是奥黛·科尼什。我们首先从双方承诺暂时停火开始。美国和伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡附近交火后，同意明天在卡塔尔会面。伊朗瞄准了美国在巴林和科威特的盟友，但一名美国官员表示，没有伊朗无人机或导弹击中预定目标，并称"明确地说，伊朗失败了"。特朗普总统威胁称，如果伊朗继续违反停火协议，它将"不复存在"。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: official says no Iranian drones or missiles hit their intended targets, saying, quote, "To be clear, Iran failed." President Trump threatened that, if Iran continued violating the ceasefire, it, quote, "will no longer exist."
paragraphs.cn: 如果伊朗政权有一秒钟认为特朗普总统会坐视不管，在伊朗继续攻击国际航运或我们的基地而不作出回应，那他们就大错特错了。过去几个晚上他们清楚地看到了这一点，我们将继续在必要时采取军事行动，摧毁他们试图用来非法控制国际水道的设施。

paragraphs.speaker: Mike Waltz
paragraphs.text: If the Iranian regime thinks for a second that President Trump is going to sit -- sit by, stand by while Iran continues to attack international shipping without a response or our bases without a response, they're sadly mistaken. And they saw that loud and clear over the last few nights, where we'll continue to militarily, if needed, take down their infrastructure that they're trying to use to illegally control an international waterway.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧。为了帮助我们今天在群聊中理清这一切，我们有《NOTUS》的国会记者伊戈尔·博比奇；彭博经济学的国防负责人贝卡·瓦瑟；《大西洋月刊》的特约撰稿人迈克尔·谢勒；以及CNN政治分析师兼Axios全国政治记者亚历克斯·汤普森。大家早上好。感谢各位的到来。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right. To help us figure this all out today in the group chat, Igor Bobic, congressional reporter with "NOTUS"; Becca Wasser, defense lead at Bloomberg Economics; Michael Scherer, staff writer at "The Atlantic"; and Alex Thompson, CNN political analyst and national political correspondent with Axios. Good morning, everybody. Thank you guys for being here.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。今天加入群聊帮助我们分析这一切的是："NOTUS"国会记者伊戈尔·博比奇、彭博经济防务主管贝卡·瓦瑟、《大西洋月刊》撰稿人迈克尔·谢勒，以及CNN政治分析师兼Axios全国政治记者亚历克斯·汤普森。大家早上好。感谢各位的到来。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, Becca, before I bring in the group, let's talk about this kind of back and forth, because there's a lot of question of when is a ceasefire not a ceasefire? At the same time, I feel like the U.S. and Iran behave similarly in some ways in terms of threats versus rhetoric.
paragraphs.cn: 那么，贝卡，在我请其他人发言之前，我们先谈谈这种反复拉锯的情况，因为有很多疑问：什么时候停火不算停火？同时，我觉得美国和伊朗在某些方面，比如威胁与言辞上，表现得很相似。

paragraphs.speaker: Becca Wasser
paragraphs.text: I think both Tehran and Washington have decided that the ceasefire is more fire than cease. They are OK with the level of violence and these skirmishes, these exchange of attacks, as they try and set their red lines and try and shape negotiations, frankly, via these attacks. And so, I think we're going to see these for the foreseeable future. And right now, there is a commitment to continue talking at the same time that they're going to continue shooting.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为德黑兰和华盛顿都认为停火更像是"停火"而非"停火"。他们接受目前的暴力程度和小规模冲突、互相攻击，因为他们试图通过这些攻击来划定红线并塑造谈判。所以，我认为在可预见的未来，我们会继续看到这种情况。而现在，他们在继续射击的同时，也承诺继续对话。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Like, the technical talks are still going.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。技术性谈判仍在进行。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And I'm under the impression this is the part that really gets into the nitty gritty of what happens to enriched uranium. Where does it go? So, they are still sort of -- it's like parallel tracks, I think.
paragraphs.cn: 我的印象是，这部分真正涉及到了浓缩铀处理的细节问题。它去了哪里？所以，他们仍然在某种程度上 --  -- 我认为是并行轨道。

paragraphs.speaker: Wasser
paragraphs.text: It's parallel tracks, but at the same time, you see Iran trying to exert its control over the Strait of Hormuz by saying that ships taking this Southern route, this U.S.-approved route via Oman, is unacceptable to them, because it is not the one that is being coordinated by Iran. So, Iran is trying to retain leverage over the Strait of Hormuz, its major lever that it has had during this war, without --
paragraphs.cn: 是并行轨道，但同时，你会看到伊朗试图通过宣称美国批准的经由阿曼的南部航线不可接受来施加对霍尔木兹海峡的控制，因为这不是伊朗协调的航线。因此，伊朗试图保持对霍尔木兹海峡的影响力，这是它在战争期间拥有的主要杠杆，而不 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Let me follow up on you saying that, because Jake Sullivan, the former national security adviser under Biden, was on FAREED ZAKARIA this weekend. He was asked this question about whether or not Iran, through this process, is actually getting concessions. Here was his read of the situation.
paragraphs.cn: 让我接着你刚才说的追问一下，因为拜登时期的前国家安全顾问杰克·沙利文本周末上了《法里德·扎卡利亚》节目。他被问及伊朗是否通过这一过程实际上获得了让步。以下是他对局势的看法。

paragraphs.speaker: Jake Sullivan
paragraphs.text: The Iranians are leaning forward to exercise control over the strait to remind the world that they control that waterway. Then, leaning back when the Trump administration objects vigorously enough, because the Iranians want to keep getting the windfall that they've gotten out of this MOU. And then when it comes to the nuclear file, I think they will dribble out very small concessions bit by bit, then pull them back, then put them forward, then pull them back to keep the United States at the table.
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗人向前倾斜，以行使对海峡的控制，提醒世界他们控制着那条水道。然后，当特朗普政府足够强烈地反对时，他们又向后倾斜，因为伊朗人想继续从这份谅解备忘录中获得意外之财。而在核问题上，我认为他们会一点一点地挤出非常小的让步，然后收回，再提出，再收回，以让美国留在谈判桌上。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK, so the U.S. wants to be at the table, but they're asking for supplemental war funding of Congress to do it to the tune of 87.6 billion. Igor, you follow Congress. As the Senate has nudged towards going against war powers, what is the mood on the Hill for paying this bill? IGOR BOBIC, CONGRESSIONAL REPORTER, "NOTUS": Not very good at all right now.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，所以美国想留在谈判桌上，但他们要求国会提供876亿美元的补充战争资金。伊戈尔，你关注国会。随着参议院倾向于反对战争权力，国会山对支付这笔账单的情绪如何？国会记者伊戈尔·博比奇（"NOTUS"）：目前非常不好。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Democrats are obviously very opposed to this to begin with, but I think the fraying of this ceasefire -- or not ceasefire or whatever you want to call it right now -- is just going to intensify questions in Congress about this huge amount of money they want.
paragraphs.cn: 民主党人显然一开始就非常反对，但我认为这种停火 --  -- 或者不是停火，不管你现在怎么称呼它 --  -- 的破裂只会加剧国会对他们想要这笔巨额资金的质疑。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But it's hard to picture them yanking money while people are in the field of battle. So, is it just to make noise, make them -- the Trump folks' life harder? Like, I don't get it.
paragraphs.cn: 但很难想象他们在人们还在战场上作战时撤回资金。所以，这只是为了制造噪音，让特朗普团队的日子更难过吗？我不明白。

paragraphs.speaker: Bobic
paragraphs.text: Absolutely, absolutely. Democrats see blood in the water, and they're going to make them pay in November for this war that's unpopular across both parties. Gas prices are up. People are paying a lot of money here. It's not popular.
paragraphs.cn: 绝对，绝对。民主党人看到了机会，他们要让共和党在11月为这场两党都不受欢迎的战争付出代价。油价上涨，人们为此付出高昂代价。这并不受欢迎。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Although gas prices are down a tiny bit, which I think is what the president wanted. Right. But certainly, by July 4th, I think they're clocking in at around -- Oh, there we go -- $3.86. In this new book, "Regime Change," they -- Maggie Haberman, coauthor, was talking about sort of who wanted to be involved in this war, who didn't want to be involved in this war, in the administration. Here's how she described it.
paragraphs.cn: 尽管油价略有下降，我认为这正是总统想要的。没错。但可以肯定的是，到7月4日，油价大概在 --  -- 哦，看这里 --  -- 3.86美元。在这本新书《政权更迭》中，合著者玛吉·哈伯曼谈到了政府内部谁想卷入这场战争，谁不想卷入。她是这样描述的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: MAGGIE HABERMAN, CO-AUTHOR, "REGIME CHANGE": In our reporting -- and we write about this in the book -- none of Trump's senior advisors, none of his cabinet, really thought this was a good idea. But Vance was the only one who really vocally took issue with this, with Trump. And it irritated Trump. It cost Vance with Trump. But he was the only person who was really sort of rattling the cages.
paragraphs.cn: 玛吉·哈伯曼，《政权更迭》合著者：根据我们的报道 --  -- 我们在书中也写到了 --  -- 特朗普的高级顾问和内阁成员中，没有人真正认为这是个好主意。但万斯是唯一一个公开对此提出异议的人，直接与特朗普唱反调。这激怒了特朗普，也损害了万斯在特朗普心中的地位。但他是唯一一个真正敢于挑战现状的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: What do you see in what's happening now in light of what she's saying about what happened when this all started?
paragraphs.cn: 鉴于她所说的这一切刚开始时的情况，你现在看到了什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Alex Thompson
paragraphs.text: I think you're seeing a larger I mean, what she is talking about is a larger dynamic in this White House, is that people do not want to give Trump bad news. And so sometimes, he is making decisions based on the good news people are trying to share with him.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为你看到的是更大的 --  -- 我的意思是，她所谈论的是白宫中更大的动态，那就是人们不想给特朗普带来坏消息。所以有时，他根据人们试图与他分享的好消息来做决定。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And if you give him the bad news, you end up holding the bag, it seems, because Vance apparently --
paragraphs.cn: 如果你给他带来坏消息，你最终似乎会背黑锅，因为万斯显然 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Thompson
paragraphs.text: I mean -- I mean exactly, like -- and it's -- and part of the reporting of this book is that Vance, his relationship with Trump was hurt by doing this. And you're seeing Trump having -- trotting -- trotting out Vance and being like, Well, now you fix it. You didn't even want to go in, but now it's your responsibility to fix this thing. MICHAEL SCHERER, STAFF WRITER, "THE ATLANTIC": There's also a shift that's happened.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是 --  -- 正是如此 --  -- 而且 --  -- 这本书的部分报道指出，万斯与特朗普的关系因此受损。而你现在看到特朗普让万斯出来，好像在说，好吧，现在你来解决。你当初甚至不想介入，但现在解决这件事是你的责任。《大西洋月刊》特约撰稿人迈克尔·谢勒：还发生了一个转变。

paragraphs.speaker: Thompson
paragraphs.text: You know, if we think back just three months ago, President Trump believed he had the hottest hand in history.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，如果我们回想三个月前，特朗普总统相信他拥有历史上最强势的地位。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: He had all the cards, as he likes to say.
paragraphs.cn: 他拥有所有的牌，正如他喜欢说的那样。

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.text: All the cards. He'd come out of Venezuela. He'd -- he'd remade the federal government. And -- and all the facts were on the table. He was presented with the reality of what it meant to go to Iran. But he just believed this was sort of his destiny. I think we're at a different stage of the presidency right now. It's very clear that this has humbled him, and he doesn't have the power, the sort of all-achieving power that he thought he had a few months ago.
paragraphs.cn: 所有的牌。他刚从委内瑞拉脱身。他 --  -- 他重组了联邦政府。而且 --  -- 所有事实都摆在桌面上。他面对的是进入伊朗意味着什么的现实。但他只是相信这似乎是他的命运。我认为我们现在处于总统任期的不同阶段。很明显，这件事让他变得谦卑，他不再拥有几个月前他认为自己拥有的那种无所不能的力量。

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.text: And so, I -- you know, there are personality conflicts all the time in the White House.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我 --  -- 你知道，白宫里一直都有性格冲突。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. But we'll talk about this more, because we've got SCOTUS rulings today.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。但我们会进一步讨论这个问题，因为今天最高法院有裁决。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Which are also going to ring fence his power. Maybe, maybe not. And then we've got the midterms. Becca, I do want to get one more word from you. It's one thing for Iran and the U.S. to -- to be going back and forth. There's somebody at the helm of a ship who has to decide to go forward or not. Are they? Are they afraid? Do they believe that they can sail through a ceasefire?
paragraphs.cn: 这些裁决也将限制他的权力。也许，也许不会。然后还有中期选举。贝卡，我想再听你说一句。伊朗和美国之间来回交锋是一回事。但掌舵的人必须决定是否前进。他们害怕吗？他们相信自己能通过停火吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Wasser
paragraphs.text: Iran can believe that they control the strait. The U.S. can believe that the strait is open. But who actually decides? It's those commercial shippers? It's the people at the helm of the ship. And some are willing to actually take a chance. And some are perhaps a little bit reticent. And that is why --
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗可以相信他们控制着海峡。美国可以相信海峡是开放的。但谁真正决定呢？是那些商业航运公司？是掌舵的人。有些人愿意冒险。有些人可能有点犹豫。这就是为什么 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. We're seeing some say there's different routes they can use.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我们看到有人说他们可以使用不同的路线。

paragraphs.speaker: Wasser
paragraphs.text: Is it safe? There may still be mines in the water. And the U.S. is still providing armed overwatch. It's still trying to get ships out. And I think we might actually end up seeing an uptick in U.S. military activity, trying to protect the ships that are trying to make it in and out of the waterway, because of President Trump's desire to make sure that oil is flowing in and out, as needed.
paragraphs.cn: 安全吗？水中可能还有水雷。美国仍在提供武装掩护。仍在试图让船只离开。我认为我们实际上可能会看到美国军事活动增加，试图保护那些试图进出水道的船只，因为特朗普总统希望确保石油按需进出。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. Becca Wasser, always love having you here. Appreciate your insight. Coming up on CNN, we're going to talk about what we mentioned earlier: the Supreme Court getting ready with their major decisions that could affect this White House. That's coming out this morning. Plus, the renovator in chief. President Trump's next project in his mission to make over D.C. And Haiti has a "do not travel" advisory, but the Trump administration is defending its decision to send some Haitians back home.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。贝卡·瓦瑟，总是很高兴你来。感谢你的见解。接下来在CNN，我们将讨论我们之前提到的：最高法院准备做出可能影响白宫的重大裁决。今天上午公布。此外，还有首席翻新官。特朗普总统改造华盛顿特区的下一个项目。海地有"不要旅行"建议，但特朗普政府为其将一些海地人送回国的决定辩护。

paragraphs.speaker: Markwayne Mullin
paragraphs.text: So, that isn't -- that -- that advisory is to American citizens traveling to Haiti, not Haitians going back home.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，那 --  -- 那个建议是针对前往海地的美国公民，而不是回国的海地人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right. It's almost 15 minutes past the hour. This is your morning roundup. Three firefighters confirmed dead, two injured as fires spread along the Colorado-Utah border. Utah's governor announcing firework restrictions ahead of July 4th, citing firefighters' strained resources. The National Weather Service issued the highest possible fire weather risk. Today, temperatures are expected to rise in the area.
paragraphs.cn: 好了。现在快到15分。这是你的早间简报。三名消防员确认死亡，两人受伤，大火沿着科罗拉多-犹他州边界蔓延。犹他州州长宣布在7月4日前限制烟花，理由是消防资源紧张。国家气象局发布了最高可能的火灾天气风险。今天，该地区气温预计将上升。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And from New York to Seattle, millions took to the streets Sunday for the annual Pride parades, marking the anniversary of the historic Stonewall uprising. The celebrations this year carried a distinct weight: joy mixed with defiance as marchers gathered against the backdrop of a widening political battle. The Trump administration actively moving to roll back transgender rights, in particular. And tennis star Serena Williams will compete at the Wimbledon tomorrow in London.
paragraphs.cn: 从纽约到西雅图，数百万人周日走上街头参加一年一度的骄傲游行，纪念历史性的石墙起义周年。今年的庆祝活动带有明显的分量：欢乐与反抗交织，游行者聚集在日益扩大的政治斗争背景下。特朗普政府尤其积极推动撤销跨性别者权利。网球明星塞雷娜·威廉姆斯明天将在伦敦参加温布尔登网球公开赛。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Williams will be facing 20-year-old Maya Joint in the first round of the competition. And Williams will also compete alongside her sister, Venus Williams, in the doubles matches at Wimbledon. And the Reflecting Pool, now blocked off by security fences ahead of the July 4th holiday as the attempts to clean it up from algae blooms continue. Security cameras have also been installed as the Trump administration alleges people have been vandalizing the pool following a multimillion-dollar project.
paragraphs.cn: 威廉姆斯将在首轮比赛中对阵20岁的玛雅·乔因特。威廉姆斯还将与姐姐维纳斯·威廉姆斯一起参加温布尔登网球公开赛的双打比赛。而倒影池在7月4日假期前已被安全围栏封锁，清理藻华的工作仍在继续。特朗普政府声称，在耗资数百万美元的项目之后，有人一直在破坏该池，因此还安装了监控摄像头。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The president also seems to be eyeing a new project: building a golf course on the Potomac River. He spent Sunday afternoon touring the East Potomac golf links and later said on social media the course would be shut down for renovations, which, weirdly, is in the group chat. Because, Michael, you were writing about this. I feel like the Reflecting Pool, golf things, they sound minor. But what do they tell us about, like, how the administration is operating?
paragraphs.cn: 总统似乎也在考虑一个新项目：在波托马克河上建造一个高尔夫球场。他周日下午参观了东波托马克高尔夫球场，随后在社交媒体上表示，该球场将关闭进行翻修，奇怪的是，这消息出现在群聊中。因为迈克尔，你之前写过这个。我觉得倒影池、高尔夫球场这些事听起来微不足道。但它们能告诉我们什么关于政府运作方式的信息呢？

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.text: It's about him. He's obsessed with this stuff. I mean, he spent all day -- all day yesterday --
paragraphs.cn: 这关乎他本人。他对这些东西着迷。我是说，他花了一整天 --  -- 昨天一整天 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, developer's going to develop. Like --
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，开发商就是要开发。就像 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.text: Yes, this is what he likes to do. You know, you mentioned earlier the -- the supplemental request for the Iran War. There's $500 million in there for the park service to rebuild the seawall around the golf course, because that golf course floods every year. Whenever --
paragraphs.cn: 是的，这就是他喜欢做的事。你知道，你之前提到过 --  -- 伊朗战争的补充拨款请求。其中有5亿美元用于公园管理局重建高尔夫球场周围的海堤，因为那个球场每年都会被淹。每当 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Just to be clear, the war funding bill --
paragraphs.cn: 需要明确的是，战争拨款法案 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- has a clause for the golf course?
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 包含高尔夫球场的条款？

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.text: Yes. And that's after he tried to get in that immigration supplemental this summer. He tried to get $1 billion for the East Wing modernization, they call it. The ballroom.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。而且那是在他试图在今年夏天将移民补充拨款纳入之后。他试图为东翼现代化改造争取10亿美元，他们称之为舞厅。

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.text: The ballroom construction.
paragraphs.cn: 舞厅建设。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, is that in or out?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，这个项目是纳入还是排除？

paragraphs.speaker: Bobic
paragraphs.text: That's -- that's out. But they're not done.
paragraphs.cn: 那 --  -- 那出局了。但他们还没完。

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.text: That was out. No. And on top of that, when he put out his budget earlier this year, there was $10 billion for D.C. beautification, which Congress has not done anything with. So, he keeps asking for more and more money for his projects. So far, even Republicans are, like, what are you talking about?
paragraphs.cn: 那出局了。不。而且除此之外，他今年早些时候提出预算时，有100亿美元用于华盛顿特区美化，国会对此没有任何行动。所以，他不断为他的项目要求更多资金。到目前为止，连共和党人都说，你在说什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But sneaking them into these, like, pieces of must-pass legislation, basically.
paragraphs.cn: 但基本上是把它们偷偷塞进这些必须通过的法案里。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It's weird, because the Reflecting Pool is supposed to reflect Washington and where Washington is. And right now, this reflection is actually pretty accurate, right? Which is, like, high fences, surveillance, and some contracts that didn't turn out quite the way the administration wanted.
paragraphs.cn: 这很奇怪，因为倒影池本应反映华盛顿及其现状。而现在，这个倒影实际上相当准确，对吧？也就是高高的围栏、监控，以及一些没有按政府预期进行的合同。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Not my usual editorializing, but it's pretty obvious.
paragraphs.cn: 这不是我通常的评论，但这很明显。

paragraphs.speaker: Thompson
paragraphs.text: They're bulldozing through red tape with all these no-bid contracts that some of whom are Trump's friends, in order to do all these things.
paragraphs.cn: 他们正在用所有这些无竞标合同（其中一些是特朗普的朋友）来冲破繁文缛节，以便做所有这些事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Thompson
paragraphs.text: And then sometimes you've got shoddy contractors doing -- doing work.
paragraphs.cn: 然后有时你会遇到劣质承包商在做 --  -- 做工作。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But on July 4th, to see it like this, it's grim.
paragraphs.cn: 但在7月4日看到这样，真是令人沮丧。

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.text: They're also taking away from the rest of the country. I mean, the -- a lot of the park, even the modernization of the Reflecting Pool, that's being paid for from national fees that otherwise would have gone to parks like Yellowstone and Yosemite. When you pay a ticket to go into one of those parks, 20 percent of the fee goes to a national pot, and he's basically drained that pot for D.C. projects.
paragraphs.cn: 他们也在从美国其他地方抽取资金。我的意思是，很多公园，甚至倒影池的现代化，都是用国家费用支付的，否则这些费用本应用于黄石和优胜美地这样的公园。当你买票进入其中一个公园时，20%的费用会进入国家资金池，而他基本上把这个资金池抽干了用于华盛顿特区的项目。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK, we're going to come back to, I don't know, something else, because that was kind of depressing. But I want to talk about what's happening in Venezuela. Give you the update there. There are rescue crews who are continuing to pull people out alive. Then, in the meantime, I'm going to turn to the World Cup. We want to know if the U.S. men's team actually has a shot this year.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧，我们待会再回来谈别的，因为刚才有点压抑。但我想谈谈委内瑞拉正在发生的事情。给你最新消息。救援人员仍在继续救出幸存者。与此同时，我要转向世界杯。我们想知道美国男队今年是否真的有希望。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And good morning to Miami, because that's where their next World Cup game will be: Friday morning between Argentina, the reigning champs, and Cape Verde. This year's Cinderella story.
paragraphs.cn: 迈阿密早上好，因为他们的下一场世界杯比赛将在那里举行：周五上午，卫冕冠军阿根廷对阵佛得角。这是今年的灰姑娘故事。

paragraphs.speaker: Andy Scholes
paragraphs.text: Do you give the U.S. any chance of winning?
paragraphs.cn: 你认为美国队有获胜的机会吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: No, no. Sorry, guys.
paragraphs.cn: 不，不。抱歉，伙计们。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Because France (ph) are better.
paragraphs.cn: 因为法国队更强。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: I'm confident that they will -- they will go to the final with all support that the people give to them. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: 我相信他们会在人民的支持下进入决赛。是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: This is not the sport for -- for the -- for any U.S. team.
paragraphs.cn: 这项运动不适合任何美国队。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right. International visitors talking smack about the U.S. Men's Team. Team USA is gearing up to take on Bosnia Wednesday in California. This is fresh off winning their group stage, and the players say they're feeling the support, at least from the local crowds.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧。国际访客在谈论美国男队。美国队正在准备周三在加州对阵波黑。他们刚刚赢得小组赛，球员们表示感受到了支持，至少来自当地观众。

paragraphs.speaker: Sergino Dest
paragraphs.text: I feel like there is pressure in a -- in a healthy type of way, because we all have belief and we -- we know that we can do it. So, you know, we want to do it, but like, pressure in a negative way, we don't really feel.
paragraphs.cn: 我觉得压力是健康的，因为我们都相信并且知道我们能行。所以，我们想做到，但消极的压力，我们真的没有感觉到。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right. Joining me now to discuss, Roger Bennett. He's the co-host of "Men in Blazers." Good morning. And I feel like there's all manner of sports idiom. Pressure makes diamonds, something, something. How much should American fans believe right now? ROGER BENNETT, CO-HOST, "MEN IN BLAZERS": Oh, they should believe absolutely deeply. What is football if it is not delusion and belief and hope and joy and wonder? Audie, this is an amazing time to be alive. The World Cup is on home turf.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。现在加入我讨论的是罗杰·贝内特，他是《穿蓝衣的男人》的联合主持人。早上好。我觉得有各种各样的体育习语。压力产生钻石，等等。美国球迷现在应该相信多少？罗杰·贝内特：哦，他们应该完全相信。如果没有妄想、信念、希望、喜悦和惊奇，足球算什么？奥迪，这是一个令人惊叹的时代。世界杯在主场举行。

paragraphs.speaker: Bennett
paragraphs.text: This team, look, if they're half as good as the women, we'd be utterly elated. They're about to play for the first time ever in a World Cup in the United States in prime time against, well, two teams, Bosnia and Herzegovina, who are very beatable. They're a very good team. They have their own strengths. They're going to drag us into a world of hurt. This is really about this team not so much winning the trophy, but winning our hearts.
paragraphs.cn: 这支球队，如果他们能有女队一半好，我们就非常高兴了。他们将首次在美国本土的世界杯黄金时段比赛，对阵两支球队，波黑和黑山，他们很容易被击败。他们是一支非常好的球队。他们有自己的优势。他们会让我们陷入痛苦。这支球队与其说是赢得奖杯，不如说是赢得我们的心。

paragraphs.speaker: Bennett
paragraphs.text: If they can get into the round of 16, be amongst the 16 best teams in the nation [SIC] --
paragraphs.cn: 如果他们能进入16强，成为全国最好的16支球队之一 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Bennett
paragraphs.text: -- they can write themselves into the epic lore of this journey, which is all we can ask for.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 他们就能将自己写入这段旅程的史诗传奇，这就是我们所能期望的一切。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, you're making it sound poetic. And what I'm hearing. though, is the journey is the destination, meaning maybe this is a reflection of how far the men's program and the effort to build up, you know, soccer infrastructure in the U.S. has finally come, compared to the women's team.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，你说得很诗意。而我听到的是，旅程就是目的地，意思是这或许反映了美国男子足球项目以及建设足球基础设施的努力，与女子队相比，终于取得了多大的进展。

paragraphs.speaker: Bennett
paragraphs.text: Yes. I mean, the women's team had Title IX as one of the great built-in advantages, which has delivered them to become the gold standard. On the men's side, they're playing against nations where football, soccer has been a national obsession for centuries. We've been very much playing catch-up. America loves a dream team. This U.S. team has really been a "dream on" team for so long. But we have the most talented group of individuals we've ever been able to call upon. We've got home turf advantage.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我的意思是，女子队拥有《第九条》这一巨大的先天优势，这使她们成为了黄金标准。而在男子方面，他们面对的是那些足球已成为国民痴迷长达几个世纪的国家。我们一直在努力追赶。美国热爱梦之队。这支美国队长期以来一直是一支"梦想"之队。但我们拥有有史以来最才华横溢的一批球员。我们还有主场优势。

paragraphs.speaker: Bennett
paragraphs.text: I was in Seattle to watch that game. It was utterly ecstatic, marching to the match with 15,000 fans. I was with Marshawn Lynch. We led the march there. That gives them an incredible advantage and they've played swaggy, buccaneering, joyous football. So, there's so much to believe in.
paragraphs.cn: 我在西雅图看了那场比赛。那真是极度狂喜，与15000名球迷一起游行前往比赛现场。我和马肖恩·林奇一起。我们带领了游行队伍。这给了他们难以置信的优势，他们踢出了潇洒、冒险、欢乐的足球。所以，有太多值得相信的东西。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right. I want to talk about victorious football. I want to talk about Lionel Messi, because, like, legendary performance right now. For you, how big a deal is this? Because to me, it's like he's great. And we knew he was going to be great, but it feels like maybe this is on a different level.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。我想谈谈胜利的足球。我想谈谈莱昂内尔·梅西，因为，就像现在传奇般的表现。对你来说，这有多重要？因为对我来说，他太棒了。我们知道他会很出色，但感觉这次可能不同凡响。

paragraphs.speaker: Bennett
paragraphs.text: I couldn't agree more. The man is 39 years of age. People thought the last World Cup, which he finally won amidst tears and wonder and a sense of great culmination, would be his last. But he's come back. He looks tiny, like he's just walked out of your local Supercuts. But when the ball comes to his feet, he walks for most of the game. He suddenly is a man transformed. No one is more alive in this planet than Lionel Messi when he's within 30 feet of the goal. And what he does then is humanly transcendent.
paragraphs.cn: 我完全同意。他已经39岁了。人们以为上一届世界杯 --  -- 他最终在泪水、奇迹和巨大的圆满感中赢得的 --  -- 会是他的最后一届。但他回来了。他看起来很小，就像刚从你家附近的理发店走出来。但当球到他脚下时，他在大部分比赛中都在行走。他突然变成了另一个人。当莱昂内尔·梅西在球门30英尺范围内时，地球上没有人比他更有活力。他所做的超越了人类极限。

paragraphs.speaker: Bennett
paragraphs.text: I don't like to be hyperbolic already, but he is, like -- it's like watching. And I think we all share this. There's a sense of global connection when he takes the ball and does what he does. And I think we all know at this point, he's just scored in his seventh straight game. His Argentina look absolutely delirious. It's like watching Mozart compose in real time. It's like watching Michelangelo sculpt in real time.
paragraphs.cn: 我不想夸大其词，但他就像 --  -- 就像在看。我想我们都认同这一点。当他拿球并做他所做的事情时，有一种全球性的联系感。而且我认为现在大家都知道，他已经连续七场比赛进球了。他的阿根廷队看起来欣喜若狂。就像在看莫扎特实时作曲。就像在看米开朗基罗实时雕刻。

paragraphs.speaker: Bennett
paragraphs.text: You get the sense, Audie, you're watching one of the greats, like Wolf Blitzer in THE SITUATION ROOM, human being who we'll all know in --
paragraphs.cn: 奥蒂，你会觉得你在看一位伟人，就像《战情室》里的沃尔夫·布利策，一个我们都会认识的人 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: What's that about not liking hyperbole?
paragraphs.cn: 不是说好不夸大其词的吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Bennett
paragraphs.text: -- three centuries' time. In three centuries' time, we'll all be -- we'll all be talking about what we're watching at this World Cup with him at the lead.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 三个世纪的时间。三个世纪后，我们都会 --  -- 我们都会谈论他带领我们观看的这届世界杯。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: We probably will. Can I ask, what's your big U.S. keepsake? Like, do you have a Waffle House jersey? Did you go to anywhere in particular where you saw good or funny U.S. culture merch?
paragraphs.cn: 很可能。我能问一下，你收藏了什么有代表性的美国纪念品吗？比如，你有华夫饼屋的球衣吗？你有没有去过什么特别的地方，看到有趣或好玩的美国文化商品？

paragraphs.speaker: Bennett
paragraphs.text: Oh, well, you know, I'm a New York City boy. I know I sound like I'm from England, but I love America like Kenny Powers loves America. I'm in Atlanta right now, because England are about to come and play one of the darlings of this tournament, Democratic Republic of Congo. I'm currently half a block away from a Waffle House. And as soon as we finish, I'm going to go and get smothered and covered.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，你知道，我是个纽约男孩。我知道我听起来像英国人，但我爱美国，就像肯尼·鲍尔斯爱美国一样。我现在在亚特兰大，因为英格兰队即将迎战本届赛事的热门球队之一 --  -- 刚果民主共和国。我离华夫饼屋只有半个街区。我们一结束采访，我就要去大吃一顿。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: That's correct. Correct response. Roger Bennett, thank you so much. We're going to talk again soon. I appreciate it.
paragraphs.cn: 没错。回答正确。罗杰·贝内特，非常感谢。我们很快会再聊。我很感激。

paragraphs.speaker: Bennett
paragraphs.text: Big, big love. Courage.
paragraphs.cn: 满满的爱。勇气。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Straight ahead on CNN THIS MORNING, we're going to check in on the weather in Kentucky. People are dealing with incredible rain and flooding. There's now some cleanup underway. Then we're going to talk about whether there is a A.I. takeover in the future for airlines. And the question of the fine line between modernizing and safety. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 接下来在《CNN今晨》节目中，我们将关注肯塔基州的天气。人们正面临暴雨和洪水的严峻考验。目前清理工作已经开始。然后我们将讨论航空公司未来是否会被人工智能接管，以及现代化与安全之间的微妙界限。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. I want to thank you for joining me on CNN THIS MORNING. It's now half past the hour. And here's what's happening right now. Four people confirmed dead after widespread flooding in Kentucky. The state's governor is saying that conditions, however, are improving. Now, this is following a declared state of emergency. Some areas received more than seven inches of rain. GOV. ANDY BESHEAR (D-KY): Really massive, devastating flooding.
paragraphs.cn: 大家早上好。我是奥蒂·科尼什。感谢您收看《CNN今晨》。现在是半点钟。以下是正在发生的事件：肯塔基州大面积洪水已确认四人死亡。该州州长表示，情况正在好转。此前该州已宣布进入紧急状态。一些地区降雨量超过七英寸。肯塔基州州长安迪·贝希尔：真是大规模、毁灭性的洪水。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: But we've got a lot of great first responders who heroically got out there and rescued people. And I think we're starting to see at least a light at the end of the tunnel.
paragraphs.cn: 但我们有很多出色的急救人员英勇地出动救援。我认为我们至少开始看到隧道尽头的光明了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, the state still faces extensive infrastructure damage and will need to rebuild over the coming months. Alex Murdaugh will appear in court today. His first hearing since the South Carolina Supreme Court overturned his double murder conviction. The judge will hear arguments for his new trial, which will include motions for a change of venue and more DNA testing of evidence. Murdaugh was convicted of killing his wife and son. This was back in 2023.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，该州仍面临广泛的基础设施损坏，未来几个月需要重建。亚历克斯·默多今天将出庭。这是南卡罗来纳州最高法院推翻其双重谋杀定罪后的首次听证会。法官将听取他新审判的辩论，包括变更审判地点和对证据进行更多DNA检测的动议。默多于2023年被判谋杀妻子和儿子。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But the state supreme court ruled a local court clerk improperly influenced the jury and overturned the conviction. And the Trump administration defending the push to return hundreds of thousands of Haitians to their homeland, even though the U.S. State Department issued a do not travel advisory for Haiti earlier this year.
paragraphs.cn: 但州最高法院裁定，一名地方法院书记员不当影响了陪审团，并推翻了定罪。特朗普政府正在为将数十万海地人遣返回国的努力辩护，尽管美国国务院今年早些时候发布了针对海地的旅行警告。

paragraphs.speaker: Markwayne Mullin
paragraphs.text: Well, that do not travel is not for Haitians. That's, do not travel for the United States, because they are kidnaping or trying to kidnap individuals from the United States. So, that isn't -- that advisory is to American citizens traveling to Haiti, not Haitians going back home.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，那个'不要旅行'的警告不是针对海地人的。那是针对美国人的，不要前往美国，因为他们正在绑架或试图绑架来自美国的人。所以，那个警告是针对前往海地的美国公民，而不是返回家园的海地人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Last week, the Supreme Court cleared the way for the administration to remove protections from Haitians and Syrians living legally in the U.S. And the search for survivors becoming more desperate in Venezuela as the death toll continues to grow. At least 1,450 people have died since last week's devastating earthquakes. Rescuers are working around the clock to pull survivors from the rubble. (VIDEO CLIP)
paragraphs.cn: 上周，最高法院为政府扫清了道路，取消了对合法居住在美国的海地人和叙利亚人的保护。而在委内瑞拉，随着死亡人数持续上升，寻找幸存者的工作变得更加紧迫。自上周毁灭性的地震以来，至少有1450人死亡。救援人员正在夜以继日地工作，从废墟中救出幸存者。（视频片段）

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: You're looking at this video shared by the president of El Salvador. He says Salvadorian crews rescued this 60-year-old woman after she was trapped under the rubble for 86 hours. CNN's Stefano Pozzebon is on the ground in Caracas. I actually paused because reading that number was so jarring. More than a thousand people dead. Can you talk about the challenges facing rescuers right now?
paragraphs.cn: 你正在观看的是萨尔瓦多总统分享的视频。他说，萨尔瓦多救援队救出了这位60岁的妇女，她在废墟下被困了86个小时。CNN的斯特凡诺·波佐本正在加拉加斯现场。我停顿了一下，因为读到那个数字太令人震惊了。超过一千人死亡。你能谈谈救援人员目前面临的挑战吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Stefano Pozzebon
paragraphs.text: Yes. And that number, unfortunately, Audie, will grow in the upcoming hours as we are now entering the fifth day since the tragedy. And so, the chances of rescuing someone and successfully rescuing someone are almost gone. Almost past our shoulders, unfortunately. We're coming to you live from the city of Caracas because I think this is, today, it's Monday morning. We're starting a new week here in Venezuela.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。不幸的是，奥蒂，这个数字在未来几小时内还会增加，因为我们现在已经进入灾难发生后的第五天。所以，成功救出某人的机会几乎没有了。几乎已经过去了，不幸的是。我们正在加拉加斯市进行直播，因为我认为今天是周一早上。我们在委内瑞拉开始了新的一周。

paragraphs.speaker: Stefano Pozzebon
paragraphs.text: And there is more of a feeling of a nation coming to understand, coming to ask itself how to -- what to do with this tragedy. How to make it. How to process it. Of course, as much as possible as it can be. Clearly, for somebody who has lost everything in that tragedy, it's terrible. But also there is a -- there is another nation surrounding it that is still trying to understand it, to come -- to come to senses with the magnitude of this catastrophe. Like I said, we spent the day yesterday in La Guaira.
paragraphs.cn: 现在更多的感觉是，这个国家开始理解，开始问自己如何应对这场悲剧。如何挺过去。如何消化它。当然，尽可能地去做到。显然，对于那些在这场悲剧中失去一切的人来说，这是可怕的。但与此同时，还有另一个国家在周围，仍在试图理解它，试图认清这场灾难的严重性。就像我说的，我们昨天在拉瓜伊拉度过了一天。

paragraphs.speaker: Stefano Pozzebon
paragraphs.text: That is the disaster zone where most of the damages have been confined and where there is still no power, no signal in most of the neighborhoods. Like I said, for someone who has lost the most important thing in life, this is the hour of pain. Take a listen. WILKER MOLAYA, FATHER SEARCHING FOR MISSING DAUGHTER (through translator): I pulled my daughter halfway out. I pulled my daughter halfway out. They're not going to move from there. I have nothing to lose. Let them kill me if they want.
paragraphs.cn: 那是受灾区域，大部分破坏都集中在那里，大多数社区仍然没有电力和信号。就像我说的，对于失去生命中最重要东西的人来说，这是痛苦的时刻。听一下。威尔克·莫拉亚，寻找失踪女儿的父亲（通过翻译）：我把女儿拉出了一半。我把女儿拉出了一半。他们不会从那里离开。我没什么可失去的了。如果他们想，就杀了我吧。

paragraphs.speaker: Pozzebon
paragraphs.text: That gentleman's daughter are joining the list of more than 1,400 people killed in this terrible double earthquake. For the rescue workers, it's, yes, it's still a race against time. Yesterday we spend the day speaking with rescue workers from L.A. County and Fairfax County in Virginia. They flew down here between Thursday night and Friday. And they were very busy trying to get to the bottom of the pile of rubble where we met them. They said that they want to keep working until everyone is accounted for.
paragraphs.cn: 那位先生的女儿加入了在这场可怕的双重地震中遇难的1400多人名单。对于救援人员来说，是的，这仍然是与时间的赛跑。昨天我们花了一天时间与来自洛杉矶县和弗吉尼亚州费尔法克斯县的救援人员交谈。他们在周四晚上到周五之间飞到这里。他们非常忙碌，试图到达我们见到他们时的那堆废墟的底部。他们说，他们想继续工作，直到每个人都找到为止。

paragraphs.speaker: Pozzebon
paragraphs.text: However, of course, the chance of finding someone alive now are really, really, really, really slim, unfortunately. Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 然而，当然，现在找到生还者的机会真的非常非常非常渺茫，不幸的是。奥蒂。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK, that's Stefano talking to us about the rescue effort in Venezuela as that death toll is rising. I want to turn to this, American Airlines flight bound for Bermuda was forced to abort its takeoff on Friday after a business jet crossed its path.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，这是斯特凡诺向我们介绍委内瑞拉的救援工作，死亡人数正在上升。我想转向这个话题，周五，一架飞往百慕大的美国航空航班在起飞时被迫中止，因为一架公务机穿过了它的跑道。

paragraphs.speaker: Tower
paragraphs.text: Four Quebec Sierra, you just crossed an active runway. Hold short of runway eight left.
paragraphs.cn: 魁北克塞拉四号，你刚刚穿过了一条活跃的跑道。在八号左跑道前停下。

paragraphs.speaker: Bizjet Pilot
paragraphs.text: You just told me to cross the runway, sir.
paragraphs.cn: 先生，您刚才让我穿越跑道。

paragraphs.speaker: Tower
paragraphs.text: No, we said Amerijet 461.
paragraphs.cn: 不，我们说的是 Amerijet 461。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The two planes were just a third of a mile away from each other at one point. The FAA now investigating how the call sign mix-up nearly led to disaster. Now, this also comes as the FAA announces its plans to launch an ambitious A.I. powered initiative. The government has partnered with Airspace Intelligence to use their A.I. programing aimed at predicting potential aircraft conflicts hours in advance. Former FAA air traffic controller Harvey Skolnick joins us now. Harvey, welcome back.
paragraphs.cn: 两架飞机一度相距仅三分之一英里。美国联邦航空管理局正在调查这次呼号混淆如何险些酿成灾难。与此同时，FAA 宣布计划启动一项雄心勃勃的人工智能驱动计划。政府已与 Airspace Intelligence 合作，利用其人工智能程序，旨在提前数小时预测潜在的飞机冲突。前 FAA 空中交通管制员 Harvey Skolnick 现在加入我们。Harvey，欢迎回来。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The good news is, I'm talking about a near miss and an aborted takeoff and not an actual crash, which a lot of times when I have you on, we're talking about. But it's a reminder of how difficult things have been when it comes to air safety. Is this A.I. thing going to help? Like, is this something where it feels like it's actually pretty necessary for this industry?
paragraphs.cn: 好消息是，我谈论的是一次险情和一次中止起飞，而不是实际坠机，而很多时候我请你来节目时我们都在谈论坠机。但这提醒我们，航空安全方面的情况一直有多艰难。这个人工智能项目会有帮助吗？比如，这是否让人觉得对这个行业来说确实很有必要？

paragraphs.speaker: Harvey Skolnick
paragraphs.text: Well, if I could -- if I could address the problem of runway incursions for just a second. This has been a problem in the FAA for -- since I can remember. And whatever they've done with regard to new equipment, to new radars, ground radars, status lights on the ground, protocols and air traffic controllers' phraseology and rules, they just seem -- there's nothing that has seemed to have worked so far. All the A.I. and artificial intelligence that's being used today still hasn't put a dent in runway incursions.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，如果我能 --  -- 如果我能先谈谈跑道入侵问题。从我记事起，这个问题就一直存在于 FAA 中。无论他们在新设备、新雷达、地面雷达、地面状态灯、协议以及空中交通管制员的术语和规则方面做了什么，似乎 --  -- 到目前为止似乎没有任何东西奏效。今天使用的所有人工智能和 AI 仍然没有减少跑道入侵。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Harvey, that's depressing.
paragraphs.cn: Harvey，这真令人沮丧。

paragraphs.speaker: I -- Cornish
paragraphs.text: I mean, can I -- like, can I press you a little bit? Like it feels as though you've got shortages, which we've talked about, with air traffic control. You've got this new technology that they say they want to start rolling out in the fall. Is there a sense of relief? Is this the rare industry where workers might say, you know what, we'll take any help we can get? Or are there real questions here that should be raised?
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，我能 --  -- 比如，我能追问你一下吗？感觉好像你提到了空中交通管制人员短缺，我们之前讨论过。他们表示想在秋季开始推出这项新技术。有没有一种如释重负的感觉？这是否是一个罕见的行业，工人们可能会说，你知道，我们愿意接受任何能得到的帮助？还是说这里存在真正需要提出的问题？

paragraphs.speaker: Skolnick
paragraphs.text: No, I think -- I think the air traffic controllers will certainly say they'll take all the help they can get. It's not as though that the FAA hasn't, over the years, continually been upgrading and introducing new equipment. And I'm sure the new software and new programs and new technologies are going to help. But in the end, I really do think that the biggest challenge to the U.S. airspace system is controller staffing. You know, I'm an advocate for air traffic controllers.
paragraphs.cn: 不，我认为 --  -- 我认为空中交通管制员肯定会说他们愿意接受所有能得到的帮助。这并不是说 FAA 多年来没有不断升级和引入新设备。我相信新的软件、程序和技术会有所帮助。但最终，我确实认为美国空域系统面临的最大挑战是管制员的人员配备。你知道，我是空中交通管制员的倡导者。

paragraphs.speaker: Skolnick
paragraphs.text: And I don't see where this is going to change the job that they do. They're still going to have to vector airplanes and when they get close to the airport, they're going to have to separate arrivals and departures, with en routes in arrivals and en routes in departures. And they still have to do the same job they've ever done. And their numbers are decreasing. People are retiring at a greater rate. And the target hiring of more -- of additional controllers seems to be lower than they first anticipated.
paragraphs.cn: 而且我看不出这会如何改变他们的工作。他们仍然需要引导飞机，当飞机接近机场时，他们仍然需要分离进港和离港航班，以及进港和离港的航路。他们仍然需要做他们一直在做的工作。而他们的人数正在减少。退休人员的比例更高。而招聘更多额外管制员的目标似乎低于他们最初的预期。

paragraphs.speaker: Skolnick
paragraphs.text: And that's the kind of thing that worries me.
paragraphs.cn: 这正是让我担心的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I'm not sure how much you know about this, but in the military they had a big problem with recruitment as well. That's way better now under this Hegseth Defense Department. When it comes to Duffy and recruiting here, what's going on? Have they changed the credentials required? Are they looking at new recruiting pools? Like, how is it going?
paragraphs.cn: 我不确定你对这个了解多少，但军队在征兵方面也遇到了大问题。现在在赫格塞斯领导的国防部领导下，情况好多了。至于达菲和这里的招聘，情况如何？他们是否改变了所需的资格条件？他们是否在寻找新的招聘来源？比如，进展如何？

paragraphs.speaker: Skolnick
paragraphs.text: So, they have -- you know, they put out this request to have people that were gamers apply for the job. And they had a huge --
paragraphs.cn: 所以，他们 --  -- 你知道，他们发出了一个请求，让游戏玩家申请这个职位。然后他们收到了大量 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Skolnick
paragraphs.text: Video gamers, right? And so, when they -- the last announcement that they had, lots and lots of people applied, but they still only were going to hire 2,000 or so. So, the amount of people that apply have nothing to do with the amount of people that they hire. And I would say this, there are programs coming online that seem to get people from college initiatives into the workforce sooner than they would have to taking people off the street and having them go to school in Oklahoma City. I hear about that.
paragraphs.cn: 电子游戏玩家，对吧？所以，当他们 --  -- 上次发布公告时，有很多很多人申请，但他们仍然只打算招聘大约2000人。所以，申请人数与招聘人数无关。我想说的是，有一些新项目正在上线，似乎能让大学毕业生比以往更早进入劳动力市场，而不是从街头招募人员并送他们去俄克拉荷马城上学。我听说过这个。

paragraphs.speaker: Skolnick
paragraphs.text: But I also hear -- I also hear people in the field telling me that the quality of train -- of the trainees just doesn't seem to meet the old standards. They don't know why, because I don't think those standards have changed. But I'll say this about training.
paragraphs.cn: 但我也听到 --  -- 我也听到现场的人告诉我，受训人员的素质似乎达不到以往的标准。他们不知道为什么，因为我认为那些标准没有改变。但关于培训，我要说一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Skolnick
paragraphs.text: The most difficult job that an air traffic controller can do is training other controllers because it's live traffic, they're standing behind someone, you want to give that person an opportunity to go as far as they can, but not so far as they make a mistake in separating airplanes, because the person that's doing the training is the one responsible for that loss of separation. So, that's very, very difficult. And controllers are training sometimes more than 50 percent of the time that they're on duty each day.
paragraphs.cn: 空中交通管制员能做的最困难的工作就是培训其他管制员，因为这是实时交通，他们站在别人身后，你想给那个人尽可能多的机会，但又不能让他们在分离飞机时犯错，因为负责培训的人要对分离失误负责。所以，这非常非常困难。管制员每天值班时，有时超过50%的时间都在进行培训。

paragraphs.speaker: Skolnick
paragraphs.text: And that's still -- we're still talking about six-day weeks and ten-hour days.
paragraphs.cn: 而且我们仍然在谈论每周工作六天、每天工作十小时。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Harvey, as always, I really appreciate you sharing what you learned for your community. Thank you so much.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。哈维，一如既往，我非常感谢你分享你为社区学到的东西。非常感谢。

paragraphs.speaker: Skolnick
paragraphs.text: Thank you for having me, Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 谢谢你邀请我，奥蒂。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, next on CNN THIS MORNING, we're going to talk about President Trump, whether or not he has overplayed his political hand. REP. THOMAS MASSIE (R-KY): It will be an absolute shellacking if they don't wake up.
paragraphs.cn: 那么，接下来在CNN今晨节目中，我们将讨论特朗普总统是否过度使用了政治手段。众议员托马斯·马西（肯塔基州共和党人）：如果他们不醒悟过来，那将是一场彻底的惨败。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Are the president's priorities becoming a liability for his party? And later on CNN, today, Luigi Mangione returns to the courtroom as his attorney discusses a possible plea deal. But is he going to take it?
paragraphs.cn: 总统的优先事项是否正在成为他所在政党的负担？稍后在CNN节目中，路易吉·曼吉奥内将重返法庭，他的律师将讨论可能的认罪协议。但他会接受吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, in just a few hours, the Supreme Court is expected to hand down decisions in some major cases as it wraps up its current term. Cases that test the boundaries of Donald Trump's presidential powers. They include challenges to mail-in ballots, transgender sports bans, and whether Trump can fire officials from independent agencies. And perhaps the most highly anticipated case is the attempt to end birthright citizenship via executive order.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，在短短几个小时内，最高法院预计将在结束当前任期时对一些重大案件作出裁决。这些案件考验着唐纳德·特朗普总统权力的边界。它们包括对邮寄选票、跨性别体育禁令的挑战，以及特朗普是否可以从独立机构解雇官员。而最受期待的案件可能是试图通过行政命令终止出生公民权。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Essentially, who gets to be an American? The move would undo more than a century of legal precedent that says babies born on American soil are automatically American citizens and could affect millions of children and their families. This is the argument from Trump.
paragraphs.cn: 本质上，谁有资格成为美国人？此举将推翻一个多世纪以来的法律先例，即在美国领土上出生的婴儿自动成为美国公民，并可能影响数百万儿童及其家庭。这是特朗普的论点。

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.text: All of this legislation, all of this, everything, having to do with birthright citizenship, it was at the end of the Civil War. The reason was it had to do with the babies of slaves and the protection of the babies of slaves. It didn't have to do with the protection of multi-millionaires and billionaires wanting to have their children get an American citizenship. It is the craziest thing I've ever seen.
paragraphs.cn: 所有这些立法，所有这一切，与出生公民权有关的一切，都是在内战结束时制定的。原因是它与奴隶的婴儿以及保护奴隶的婴儿有关。它与保护想要让子女获得美国公民身份的千万富翁和亿万富翁无关。这是我见过的最疯狂的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Group chat is back. As I said, this is one of a couple of complex cases. Some of them are about the power of the presidency, right? One of them, Trump v Slaughter, independent agency leadership. The president wants to overturn the precedent of whether you can fire people with cause or not. And then there are these ones that culturally are kind of ingrained. My baby, born here, in an American hospital, is automatically a citizen, which is part of a longer term conservative project, no.
paragraphs.cn: 群聊回来了。正如我所说，这是几个复杂案件之一。其中一些涉及总统的权力，对吧？其中之一是特朗普诉斯劳特案，涉及独立机构领导层。总统想要推翻关于是否可以因故解雇人员的先例。然后还有一些在文化上根深蒂固的案件。我的孩子，在这里，在美国医院出生，自动成为公民，这是长期保守派计划的一部分，不是吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: MICHAEL SCHERER, STAFF WRITER, "THE ATLANTIC": Yes. And almost with -- certainly what's going to happen this week is it's going to be a split decision for the president. And it'll be interesting to see, the last few weeks, every time a decision has come out to hurt one party or the other, they attack the court as being somehow illegitimate or wrong. And this is --
paragraphs.cn: 《大西洋月刊》特约撰稿人迈克尔·谢勒：是的。几乎可以肯定，本周将会发生的是总统将面临一个分裂的裁决。有趣的是，过去几周，每当一个裁决伤害到某一方时，他们就会攻击法院，认为它在某种程度上不合法或错误。而这 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. And the court saved them all for this week before they leave so they can play (ph), good luck.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。法院把他们所有的案件都留到了本周，在他们离开之前，这样他们就可以玩（音），祝你好运。

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.text: Right. And they're going to throw --
paragraphs.cn: 对。他们会抛出 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.text: They're going to throw a punch. I mean the betting money is that birthright citizenship, the president's going to lose. He'll win on transgender sports. He probably wins on the -- on the -- on the independent federal agencies.
paragraphs.cn: 他们会出拳。我的意思是，赌注是出生公民权，总统会输。他会在跨性别体育问题上赢。他很可能在独立联邦机构问题上赢。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Hold on. Let me get all my legal tabs here. So, you just mentioned transgender sports. This one's West Virginia state will decide if state bans on transgender students playing in girls' sports are permitted under the Constitution in federal law. What are you watching, Alex?
paragraphs.cn: 是的。等等。让我打开我所有的法律标签。你刚才提到了跨性别体育。这个案件是西弗吉尼亚州将决定州禁止跨性别学生参加女子体育项目是否符合宪法和联邦法律。你在关注什么，亚历克斯？

paragraphs.speaker: Alex Thompson
paragraphs.text: There is -- Democratic and Republican strategists are really watching a campaign finance case which the Supreme Court could further roll back restrictions on what the big parties can spend. And the upshot of this is that it would give the Republican National Committee a huge advantage in this midterm cycle, because the RNC has a ton more money, cash on hand, than the DNC, which is still in debt.
paragraphs.cn: 有 --  -- 民主党和共和党的策略师们真正关注的是一个竞选资金案件，最高法院可能进一步放宽对大党支出的限制。其结果是，这将给共和党全国委员会在中期选举周期中带来巨大优势，因为共和党全国委员会手头现金比仍处于债务中的民主党全国委员会多得多。

paragraphs.speaker: Thompson
paragraphs.text: And so, you could end up seeing a surge of over $100 million pour into those Republican races depending on what the Supreme Court does.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，根据最高法院的裁决，你可能会看到超过1亿美元的资金涌入那些共和党竞选活动中。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, it's funny, we're focusing on these things like Federal Reserve independence, right, or birthright citizenship. And meanwhile, something like mail-in ballots, Watson versus RNC. Reviewing state laws that allow election officials to receive mail-in ballots after election day. Meaning, you've sent it in, you've postmarked it before election day, but it doesn't arrive until election day. This is also extremely important.
paragraphs.cn: 有趣的是，我们关注的是美联储独立性或出生公民权这类问题。而与此同时，像邮寄选票、沃森诉共和党全国委员会案等事情，正在审查允许选举官员在选举日后接收邮寄选票的州法律。也就是说，你在选举日前寄出并盖了邮戳，但选票直到选举日才到达。这也极其重要。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Especially in the context of what you're talking about, the spending. IGOR BOBIC, CONGRESSIONAL REPORTER, "NOTUS": It's hugely important. It's a big priority of the president, who wants to limit these mail-in ballots. We've seen him complain about the California elections, where, obviously, you can send in a mail-in ballot late. And I think that they're trying to use this as a -- as a pre-November sort of setup to help them win these elections, Republicans are.
paragraphs.cn: 尤其是结合你提到的支出问题。国会记者伊戈尔·博比克表示：这非常重要。这是总统的一个重点优先事项，他希望限制这些邮寄选票。我们看到他对加州选举提出抱怨，在那里显然可以晚寄出邮寄选票。我认为他们正试图利用这一点作为11月前的某种铺垫，以帮助共和党赢得选举。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I think it's going to be a hugely -- a huge case to watch here this week.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为这将是本周一个非常重要的案件。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I'm going to turn to the elections in one second, but I want to ask you, Michael, one more thing. This is Mike Johnson talking about why he thinks birthright citizenship should end. Want you guys to hear it. REP. MIKE JOHNSON (R-LA): Like all good things, it can be abused. And birthright citizenship goes back to the, you know, root of the country, the history of the tradition. You look at the original intent of the Constitution and the founders and what they were doing, of course.
paragraphs.cn: 我马上要谈到选举，但迈克尔，我想再问你一件事。这是迈克·约翰逊在谈论为什么他认为出生公民权应该终止。请听一下。众议员迈克·约翰逊（路易斯安那州共和党人）：像所有美好的事物一样，它也可能被滥用。出生公民权可以追溯到国家的根源、历史和传统。当然，你要看宪法和开国元勋们的原始意图以及他们当时在做什么。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: They were facing a very different set of circumstances than we're facing now. We know that it's been abused in recent years because people have literally just come over the border just to have a baby. I don't think it's inconsistent at all. I think we can celebrate immigration, legal immigration. We are a nation of immigrants, as we all recognize.
paragraphs.cn: 他们当时面临的情况与我们现在截然不同。我们知道近年来它被滥用，因为人们真的只是越过边境来生孩子。我认为这完全不矛盾。我们可以庆祝移民，合法移民。我们是一个移民国家，这是我们都承认的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So this is, for them, always couched in the immigration conversation. Does anyone on this panel have kids other than me? Anyone?
paragraphs.cn: 所以对他们来说，这总是被置于移民讨论的语境中。这个小组里除了我还有人孩子吗？有人吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. Do you remember being in the hospital? And do you remember thinking to yourself, I really want to fill out citizenship documents right now? Like, why would you piss off a whole bunch of parents before the election?
paragraphs.cn: 好吧。你还记得在医院的时候吗？你还记得当时在想，我现在真的很想填写公民身份文件吗？就像，为什么要在选举前惹恼一大群父母呢？

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.text: The hilarious part about that clip is I'm old enough to remember when conservatives were about the original text of the Constitution. It wasn't a living and breathing document. His argument there is basically, because we have different people and new ways of travel, and people are coming into the U.S. that --
paragraphs.cn: 那段视频最搞笑的部分是，我年纪大到还记得保守派曾经主张宪法的原始文本。它不是一份活的、不断演变的文件。他的论点基本上是，因为我们现在有不同的人、新的旅行方式，人们正在进入美国 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: We need to interpret it based on the now.
paragraphs.cn: 我们需要根据当下情况来解释它。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Which some Democrats would love to be hearing as an interpretation.
paragraphs.cn: 一些民主党人很乐意听到这样的解读。

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.text: Right. But as -- yes, but we're just going to change what the Constitution means.
paragraphs.cn: 没错。但是 --  -- 是的，但我们就是要改变宪法的含义。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I mean originalist means something to someone. OK, I want to talk about one other thing. We were talking about the mail-in ballots thing. We were talking about the campaign finance thing. Over all of this, the Republican lawmakers who are on their way out, they are the loudest in issuing new warnings to Donald Trump, saying, look, his priorities are going to cost the GOP the midterms.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，原旨主义对某些人来说是有意义的。好的，我想谈另一件事。我们刚才在讨论邮寄选票的事情，还有竞选资金的问题。在这些问题上，即将离任的共和党议员们是最积极地向唐纳德·特朗普发出新警告的，他们说，听着，他的优先事项会让共和党在中期选举中付出代价。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The latest concerns are spilling into the open after President Trump refused to sign a bipartisan housing bill at the last-minute last week. Why? Well, because he wants Congress to pass his controversial bill aimed at controlling elections first. Over the last few days, Senator Bill Cassidy, Congressman Thomas Massie, both lost their primaries to Trump-backed challengers. And they're like, look, he needs new focus. SEN. BILL CASSIDY (R-LA): Well, I can't tell you what his priorities are beyond the SAVE Act.
paragraphs.cn: 上周，特朗普总统在最后一刻拒绝签署一项两党住房法案后，最新的担忧浮出水面。为什么？因为他希望国会先通过他旨在控制选举的争议性法案。过去几天，参议员比尔·卡西迪和众议员托马斯·马西都在初选中输给了特朗普支持的挑战者。他们表示，他需要新的重点。参议员比尔·卡西迪（路易斯安那州共和党人）说："除了《拯救法案》，我无法告诉你他的优先事项是什么。"

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I can tell you what I think they should be. And how do we make life more affordable for the average American? I voted to uphold the Constitution. The president was offended by that. REP. THOMAS MASSIE (R-KY): The problem is not the elections. We won the damn elections. The problem is, we're wasting our opportunity that the voters gave us. And the Republicans are going to pay for that in November. It will be an absolute shellacking if they don't wake up.
paragraphs.cn: 我可以告诉你我认为应该是什么。我们如何让普通美国人的生活更负担得起？我投票支持维护宪法。总统对此感到不满。众议员托马斯·马西（肯塔基州共和党人）说："问题不在于选举。我们赢了该死的选举。问题在于，我们浪费了选民给我们的机会。共和党人将在11月为此付出代价。如果他们不醒悟过来，那将是一场彻底的惨败。"

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right, group chat is back. There's a world of Republicans who say, look, we shouldn't listen to the shellacked about shellackings. These are people who struggled against Trump-appointed -- what's the word in Congress? Are they scared? Are they frustrated? Because there's always the dudes who speak out, and then there's the people who talk to you in the hallways.
paragraphs.cn: 好了，群聊回来了。有一群共和党人说，听着，我们不应该听那些惨败的人谈论惨败。这些人曾与特朗普任命的人斗争 --  -- 在国会里该怎么说？他们害怕吗？他们沮丧吗？因为总有一些人公开表态，还有一些人在走廊里跟你说话。

paragraphs.speaker: Bobic
paragraphs.text: Well, yes, I call them the YOLO caucus. You only live once caucus.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，是的，我称他们为YOLO党团。你只活一次党团。

paragraphs.speaker: Bobic
paragraphs.text: The guys who actually have the freedom to talk. But listen, Cassidy may be out front there on this, but a whole lot of Republicans I speak to agree with him.
paragraphs.cn: 那些真正有言论自由的人。但听着，卡西迪可能在这件事上冲在前面，但我交谈过的很多共和党人都同意他的看法。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, that's the yellow caucus.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，那是黄色党团。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: They're the ones who are going to speak publicly.
paragraphs.cn: 他们是那些会公开发言的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Bobic
paragraphs.text: Yes. I mean, they see a president who's more focused on, you know, renovating a golf course, as opposed to signing a housing bill, a huge bipartisan housing bill that passed Congress overwhelmingly, that still hasn't gotten signed. Why?
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我的意思是，他们看到一位总统更专注于翻新高尔夫球场，而不是签署一项住房法案 --  -- 一项在国会获得压倒性支持的两党住房法案，至今仍未签署。为什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Right. Well, to rob Democrats and a Senate that he's frustrated with of the photo op.
paragraphs.cn: 没错。嗯，是为了剥夺民主党和他不满的参议院拍照的机会。

paragraphs.speaker: Thompson
paragraphs.text: I think -- I think the fear with Republicans, it's actually much more petulant than that. And that it was a completely emotional decision by Trump because he is obsessed with the SAVE act and he denied his party wins. And, you know, Republicans are basically resigned at this point that Donald Trump is just never going to be on message throughout the midterms. He's not going to be out front talking about specific policies to bring down prices.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为 --  -- 我认为共和党人的担忧实际上比这更任性。这是特朗普完全情绪化的决定，因为他痴迷于《拯救法案》，并否定了自己政党的胜利。而且，你知道，共和党人基本上已经认命，认为唐纳德·特朗普在整个中期选举期间永远不会按套路出牌。他不会站出来谈论降低物价的具体政策。

paragraphs.speaker: Thompson
paragraphs.text: And what you're seeing in some ways is a second term Trump that just has an exhausted legislative agenda.
paragraphs.cn: 从某些方面看，你看到的是一个第二任期的特朗普，其立法议程已经枯竭。

paragraphs.speaker: Thompson
paragraphs.text: They have not really proposed any big things.
paragraphs.cn: 他们实际上没有提出任何重大举措。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But is it because he knows he can rile up his supporters and really rally them around pet culture war issues? So, one of them, of course, is the SAVE Act, which does a myriad of other things other than voting. There's a lot of stuff stuck in there. Transgender sports. And then telling states, turn in all your voter rolls because we've got to compare notes. There's a lot of question marks there. Here's Senator Tillis on Sunday, again, part of this wounded bear caucus you're talking about.
paragraphs.cn: 但这是否因为他知道如何煽动支持者，让他们围绕他偏爱的文化战争议题团结起来？所以，其中一个议题当然是《拯救法案》，该法案除了投票之外还涉及许多其他事情。里面塞了很多东西。跨性别体育。然后要求各州上交所有选民名单，因为我们要核对信息。这里有很多问号。这是周日参议员蒂利斯的部分发言，他属于你提到的"受伤熊派"。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But here's how he sees this focus. SEN. THOM TILLIS (R-NC): Why are we doing more things to undermine our confidence in elections, rather than getting the strong message out that we'll win for Republicans this year? Talk about the emergence and the rise of the Democratic Socialists of America. Accept that the voting laws are going to be fundamentally what they are today. Win by them. Win buy the good results that Republicans have produced and stop undermining the confidence in the elections.
paragraphs.cn: 但他是这样看待这个焦点的。参议员汤姆·蒂利斯（北卡罗来纳州共和党人）：为什么我们要做更多破坏选举信心的事情，而不是发出强有力的信息，表明我们今年将为共和党赢得胜利？谈谈美国民主社会主义者的崛起。接受选举法基本上会保持现状。按照这些法律取胜。凭借共和党取得的良好成果取胜，停止破坏选举信心。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The argument is not crazy, considering that just over the weekend a bunch of Democrats released a promise to America, we are capitalist, not socialist. Manifesto, if you will.
paragraphs.cn: 这个论点并不疯狂，考虑到就在上周末，一群民主党人发布了一份《对美国承诺》：我们是资本主义者，不是社会主义者。可以说是宣言。

paragraphs.speaker: Thompson
paragraphs.text: A capitalist manifesto.
paragraphs.cn: 一份资本主义宣言。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, a capitalist manifesto, ironically. But does it show that even though Trump's not on message, Democrats are not exactly getting to November together?
paragraphs.cn: 是的，讽刺的是，一份资本主义宣言。但这是否表明，尽管特朗普不按套路出牌，民主党人也并非完全团结一致地走向11月？

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.text: Yes, everything's not going well for the Democrats right now for a number of reasons. I mean, the problem we in -- they have in Maine as well as a -- is an issue. I think --
paragraphs.cn: 是的，由于多种原因，民主党目前的情况并不顺利。我的意思是，我们在缅因州以及他们面临的问题是一个问题。我认为 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
